learning_objectives.topic: Types and mathematical formulation of boundary conditions for inlets, outlets, and walls
learning_objectives.equation: ∇p·n = 0 for outlet, U = U_in for inlet
learning_objectives.importance: Defines the problem domain and ensures physical realism; incorrect BCs lead to divergence or non-physical results

learning_objectives.topic: Boundary condition framework for the CFD engine
Integration of boundary conditions into the linear system during matrix assembly
learning_objectives.challenge: Handling Dirichlet and Neumann conditions in LDU matrix without destroying sparsity or symmetry

theory_sections.title_th: ประเภทพื้นฐานของเงื่อนไขขอบเขต

equations.name: Dirichlet (Fixed Value)
equations.explanation: Value specified directly at boundary, e.g., fixed velocity at inlet or fixed temperature at wall

equations.name: Neumann (Fixed Gradient)
equations.explanation: Gradient specified normal to boundary, e.g., zero gradient for pressure at outlet or heat flux at wall

equations.name: Robin (Mixed)
equations.explanation: Combination of value and gradient, used for convective boundaries or coupled conditions

variables.name: boundary value
variables.unit: depends on field (e.g., m/s for U)

theory_sections.warning: Mixing Dirichlet and Neumann incorrectly can lead to ill-posed problems; ensure compatibility with physics (e.g., mass conservation)

theory_sections.title_th: เงื่อนไขขอบเขตทางเข้า

equations.name: Fixed Velocity Inlet
equations.explanation: Velocity specified; pressure extrapolated from interior using zero gradient

equations.name: Fixed Pressure Inlet
equations.explanation: Pressure specified; velocity gradient zero, allowing flow to adjust

equations.name: Mass Flow Inlet
equations.explanation: Mass flow rate specified; velocity computed iteratively to match flux

theory_sections.title_th: เงื่อนไขขอบเขตทางออก

equations.name: Pressure Outlet
equations.explanation: Pressure fixed (often atmospheric); velocity gradient zero, assuming fully developed flow

equations.name: Outflow (Zero Gradient)
equations.explanation: All variables have zero normal gradient; suitable for regions where flow exits smoothly

equations.name: Convective Outlet
equations.explanation: Advective condition to prevent reflections in transient simulations

theory_sections.title_th: เงื่อนไขขอบเขตผนัง

equations.name: No-Slip Wall
equations.explanation: Velocity zero at wall for viscous flows; applies to momentum equations

equations.name: Slip Wall
equations.explanation: No normal flow; zero tangential stress for inviscid or symmetry boundaries

equations.name: Wall Functions for Turbulence
equations.explanation: Model near-wall turbulence without resolving viscous sublayer; compute shear stress from log-law

equations.name: Adiabatic Wall
equations.explanation: Zero heat flux; Neumann condition for energy equation

openfoam_analysis.purpose: Manages boundary fields for volField and surfaceField; stores a list of patch fields

key_members.name: boundaryField_
key_members.purpose: List of patch fields, one per boundary patch

key_methods.name: evaluate()
key_methods.purpose: Update boundary values based on internal field and patch conditions

key_methods.name: correctBoundaryConditions()
key_methods.purpose: Apply boundary conditions, often called after field solves

openfoam_analysis.purpose: Base class for boundary conditions on patches; defines interface for setting values and matrix coefficients

key_members.name: patch_
key_members.purpose: Reference to the mesh patch this BC applies to

key_members.name: internalField_
key_members.purpose: Reference to internal field for coupling

key_methods.name: updateCoeffs()
key_methods.purpose: Update coefficients for matrix assembly, e.g., source terms from BCs

key_methods.name: evaluate()
key_methods.purpose: Set boundary field values based on current internal field and BC type

key_methods.name: manipulateMatrix(fvMatrix<Type>&)
key_methods.purpose: Modify matrix coefficients to enforce boundary condition

derived_classes.name: mixedFvPatchField
derived_classes.purpose: Robin condition: mix of value and gradient

openfoam_analysis.purpose: Represents a boundary patch in the mesh; provides geometric information like face indices and normals

key_members.name: start_
key_members.purpose: Starting face index in the mesh face list

key_members.name: size_
key_members.purpose: Number of faces in this patch

key_members.name: Sf_
key_members.purpose: Face area vectors (magnitude = area, direction = normal)

classes.name: BoundaryCondition
classes.responsibility: Abstract base class for all boundary conditions in the CFD engine

key_methods.name: applyToMatrix(lduMatrix&, Field<scalar>&)
key_methods.purpose: Modify matrix diagonal, off-diagonal, and source for boundary cells

key_methods.name: applyToField(volScalarField&)
key_methods.purpose: Set boundary field values after solve

key_methods.name: update()
key_methods.purpose: Update BC parameters if they depend on solution (e.g., for coupled problems)

classes.name: BoundaryConditionManager
classes.responsibility: Manage all boundary conditions for the solver; centralizes BC application

key_methods.name: addCondition(const string& patchName, const string& fieldName, BoundaryCondition* bc)
key_methods.purpose: Register a boundary condition for a specific patch and field

key_methods.name: applyConditionsToMatrix(const string& fieldName, lduMatrix& mat, Field<scalar>& source)
key_methods.purpose: Apply all BCs for a field during matrix assembly

key_methods.name: applyConditionsToField(const string& fieldName, volScalarField& field)
key_methods.purpose: Set boundary values after solving the field

key_methods.name: readFromDict(const dictionary&)
key_methods.purpose: Initialize BCs from input dictionary (e.g., 'boundaryField' in OpenFOAM)

algorithm_steps: 1. Read boundary condition specifications from input dictionary (mimicking OpenFOAM's system/controlDict or constant/boundaryField)
2. Initialize BoundaryCondition objects for each patch and field (e.g., U, p, T, alpha)
3. During matrix assembly for each equation: call BoundaryConditionManager::applyConditionsToMatrix() to modify coefficients
4. After solving the linear system: call BoundaryConditionManager::applyConditionsToField() to enforce BCs on the solution field
5. In outer solver loop: update() BCs if they are solution-dependent (e.g., for phase change interfaces)
6. Ensure consistency: e.g., for velocity-pressure coupling, apply BCs in correct order (velocity then pressure)

concept_checks.question: เหตุใดจึงต้องระบุเงื่อนไขขอบเขตสำหรับ pressure ที่ outlet ในการไหลแบบ incompressible?
answer_key_points: Pressure is a relative quantity in incompressible flows; absolute value is not unique
Specifying pressure at outlet sets a reference point for the entire domain
Without a pressure reference, the solution is indeterminate up to an additive constant
In practice, fixedValue or zeroGradient at outlet stabilizes the pressure equation
concept_checks.implementation_note: In Solver::solvePressure(), ensure outlet BC modifies matrix to enforce p_b = p_out or ∂p/∂n = 0

concept_checks.question: Wall functions ใช้ในกรณีใดและช่วยลด computational cost อย่างไร?
answer_key_points: ใช้เมื่อมี turbulent flow ใกล้ผนัง ที่ Reynolds number สูง
ช่วยลดจำนวนเซลล์ที่ต้อง resolve viscous sublayer ซึ่งต้องการ mesh ละเอียดมาก
แทนที่จะ resolve all the way to wall, ใช้ empirical log-law to compute shear stress
ลดจำนวน cells ได้ถึง 10-100 เท่า สำหรับ near-wall regions
concept_checks.implementation_note: Implement in momentum BCs by modifying wall shear stress based on y+ and u+ from log-law

concept_checks.question: การกำหนด fixedValue ที่ inlet สำหรับ velocity และ zeroGradient ที่ outlet สำหรับ pressure ถูกต้องหรือไม่? ทำไม?
answer_key_points: ถูกต้องสำหรับหลายกรณี เช่น internal flows with specified inflow
Fixed velocity inlet กำหนด flow rate, zeroGradient pressure outlet กำหนด pressure reference
ต้องตรวจสอบว่า flow direction สอดคล้อง (e.g., no backflow at outlet)
อาจต้องใช้ pressure outlet แทนหากมี backflow risk

pitfalls.symptom: Solver diverges or gives unrealistic backflow at outlet
pitfalls.cause: Using zeroGradient outlet for velocity in developing or recirculating flow
pitfalls.fix: Switch to pressure outlet with fixed pressure and zero velocity gradient, or use convective outlet condition

pitfalls.symptom: Pressure field oscillates wildly or solution fails to converge
pitfalls.cause: Inconsistent boundary conditions, e.g., fixed pressure at both inlet and outlet without compatible velocity BCs, or missing reference pressure
pitfalls.fix: Ensure only one pressure reference point (usually outlet), and set velocity BCs to allow mass conservation (e.g., fixed velocity inlet with computed pressure gradient)

pitfalls.symptom: Wall shear stress computed incorrectly in turbulent flows
pitfalls.cause: Not applying wall functions when using coarse mesh near wall, leading to over-prediction of friction
pitfalls.fix: Implement wall function BCs for turbulence variables (e.g., k, epsilon) and momentum, based on y+ values

connections.previous_day: Day 05: Mesh Topology (owner-neighbor addressing; boundary faces are owned by interior cells, critical for applying BCs in matrix)
connections.next_day: Day 07: Linear Algebra (solving matrix systems; BCs modify the LDU matrix coefficients and RHS vector)
connections.key_link: Today's BC implementation directly feeds into Day 07's linear solver by defining how boundary contributions are incorporated into the matrix (e.g., modifying diagonal for Dirichlet conditions)
